title: Competitor Intelligence
description: Research competitors on the web, create a strategic brief, and save findings to Notion. Exercises DuckDuckGo search and Notion integration.

nodes.title: Competitor Names
nodes.prompt: Ask the user: which competitors should we research? Suggest: (A) Enter competitor names or URLs, (B) Describe your market and I'll find competitors, (C) Upload a list.

humanQuestions.question: How should we choose the competitors to research?

options.label: Use specific competitors
options.description: Type competitor names or URLs in the composer.

options.label: Discover them from my market
options.description: Describe the market and audience in the composer.

options.label: Use an existing list
options.description: Paste the list or attach the source file.

humanQuestions.question: What names, URLs, market description, or list should the research use?

nodes.title: Deep Research
nodes.prompt: Research each competitor. Find: positioning, content strategy, audience engagement, product offering, pricing signals, social presence. Record URLs and dates.

nodes.title: Intel Brief
nodes.prompt: From the research, write a competitive intel brief: each competitor's positioning, content approach, strengths, gaps, and opportunities for differentiation.

nodes.title: Review Intel
nodes.prompt: Show the intel brief to the user. Ask: (A) Approve and save to Notion, (B) Edit and then save, (C) Reject and refine research.

humanQuestions.question: What should happen to the competitive intelligence brief?

options.label: Approve and save
options.description: Save the current brief to Notion.

options.label: Refine the research
options.description: Return to research instead of saving.

nodes.title: Save to Notion
nodes.prompt: Create a new page in the Competitor Intelligence database. Include: competitor name, positioning summary, strengths, gaps, opportunities, and research date. Return the page URL.
